text1: "The space-time shear effect appears to be approaching, but not quite, propagating at local light speeds. Still working on this, ruling out sensor calibration errors. Can't speculate on the theoretical range; the limiting factor is the ability to focus in normal space. The damage to unhardened targets is significant. I'll have to write a formal report later, we have so much new data coming in... Not sure how these robots can do this on their own. Zunya would love this."
text2: - Sealed holographic report submitted only to Special Purchasing Executive

text1:
A sign on the bridge identifies the ship as a product of the Ziggurat Project. Apparently, she was born in a Tri-Tachyon prototype laboratory unknown to the people of Persean. In addition to the advanced armament, the ship also has some special components that cannot be produced routinely. Although it can restore intact samples and produce spare parts through reverse engineering, its internal working principle cannot be found in any remaining "Domain of Man" technical data. No reference or similar structure can be found, leaving a mystery that may never be solved.

The Ziggurat-class once had a human crew, or at least appeared to be a warship that needed a human crew. However, all traces or records of their existence are completely nowhere to be found. Even the cleanest Hegemony ships will have someone's initials on the back cover of the maintenance access, or a casual comment or two about their superiors. But where they should be, there is nothing.
text3: The readings from the target signal showed that the ship was equipped with a phase function, but theoretically it was impossible for such a huge ship to be equipped with a phase device due to practical stability issues. The sensor detected capital ship-level weapons and equipment, and at the same time, a large amount of abnormal data showed that the ship was also equipped with at least one unknown system. Tactical analysis equipment was unable to identify the role of this system. Recommendation: Approach targets with caution.

According to the experienced and well-known unlucky guys in the tavern, as well as the captains who can only be seen often in the outer network star area, Tri-Tachyon once produced a small amount of a super frigate named Noble. Yes, it was the one whose reward in the bounty has never fallen out of the top 20. Many people Everyone thought this was just a rumor... until you saw this thing. The personal terminal produced by the "Supreme Unit" Tri-Tachyon displays the name of this unit like this. This means that at least one unlucky person encountered this and named it this way. Now that you have encountered it, your personal terminal automatically queried this thing.

It seems that the lucky ones who succeeded in getting the other noble and incomparable ones and those guys with big eyes and hands must have fallen into the hands of these strange automatic ships, and it is difficult to say whether these things are even intelligent machines and they have successfully copied the incomparable noble core system. This is not good news.

When you first saw this ship called the Parliamentary Unit, you thought it was a specially painted Morgul. After all, the Morgul was too expensive for Diable Avionics, but after the complete scan report came out, you broke out in a cold sweat. Yes, this was a Morgul, and its main frame, propulsion center, and other details were no different from a Morgul. However, it had no cabins for personnel, no equipment that required personnel, and even the bridge had been heavily modified and covered with various external sensors.

This means that at least one fleet with a Morgul was bombed and sunk in the starry sky by these automatic ships, and Diable Avionics did not even come to recover these expensive Morgul-class dreadnoughts. What’s worse is that your technical officer told you that the sensor scanned an energy response similar to the original Morgul-1, but the intensity was higher. The special systems of its ships must have been greatly enhanced. The situation is very bad.

Have you heard about the rumors about Diable Avionics that there are more than one Sage and Wanze class, but you have never seen more Sage and Wanze class outside the main galaxy of Diable Avionics. But when you see this thing called the prophecy unit, you will know that there are reasons for those long-lasting rumors. For example, it is not a rumor, but a fact. At first, you thought this strange large ship The humanoid flying object is the product of a strange blueprint error. Until your fleet further scans this ship, it has two strange weapons. In theory, these special weapons can only be built into the hull, but the structure on it shows that it has the possibility of replacement. After further scanning, you find that it has a very similar structure to the Sage and Wanze class, and the details are even better than the Diable Avionics.

It seems that Diable Avionics thought it was fighting these automatic ships for some reason, and then a Sage and Wanze class was blown to pieces? Or maybe a fleet with Sage and Wanze class has been fighting them for many years, so that they already have enough data to simulate something similar?

It is difficult to determine whether this is a creation of the Dominion of Man. Although the operating system on it is indeed a human voice, its design is completely contrary to the naval warfare doctrine of the Dominion of Man. There are also a large number of incomprehensible spatial anomalies and anti-entropy phenomena, and the internal layout is not fixed.
-Captain of a certain Tri-Tachyon phase fleet

An extremely advanced autocannon that fires ammunition that detonates into a cloud of nanobots on approach, overloading the target's shields and cannibalizing sections of its hull.

"This is the ultimate result after we reverse-engineered the ancient relic 'Purgatory Ray'. By injecting high-dimensional energy directly into the core of the ship, we successfully replicated that forbidden moment - a blue-violet beam capable of tearing apart the fabric of reality, accompanied by the overload resonance of the shield matrix, burning the enemy ship and the space around it. Each activation is a brief override of the laws of physics, and a trial in which the ship itself is sacrificed."

——Chief Alien Technology Researcher, File No. 7

During the heyday of the Domain of Man, a forgotten automated industrial hub quietly awakened on the edge of the core the Sector. Its core instruction set has degenerated over the years into a string of primitive codes: Make, keep making. When the first exploration teams discovered it, the entire star area had been transformed into a silent factory by the nanomachines - the planets were stripped into raw materials, the asteroid belt turned into a river of slurry, and the light of the star was obscured by the honeycomb structure in orbit.

All the forces in Persean respond with deep hatred to it. The Hegemony regarded it as the most shameful betrayal of the Domain of Man's legacy; Tri-Tachyon found an AI architecture in its database that far exceeded contemporary AI architecture, but could not decipher it; the Luddic Church called it a "mechanical plague." But every joint siege failed - it repaired itself like a living celestial body, devouring the debris and multiplying more combat units. The defense line has retreated again and again, and now the core the Sector has been reduced to the last fortress, and the "control head" is still hidden deep under the layers of fortifications, mocking the limits of flesh and blood with desperate productivity.

No one knows whether it still retains the consciousness of the human realm. In the shortwave signals occasionally intercepted by the sensor, only three phrases are repeated: "Threat elimination", "Stop manufacturing"... and an identity code that has long been erased by history.

Chaotic AI Core, RESOURCE," In an era when the Domain of Man's restrictive laws on AI cores were still regarded as the norm, this level of autonomous intelligence was never allowed to be born. However, in the ruins of a forgotten laboratory, a group of desperate technical geeks used broken blueprints and out-of-control nanoforges to recompile a piece of abandoned Beta-level core code and inject overloaded defense protocols. The result: a paranoid, almost pathologically obsessed artificial soul with self-preservation.

When managing colony facilities, the deranged AI core prioritizes all resources for hardening fortifications and redundant survival systems, far more efficiently than any human manager, but at the cost of a significant decrease in other output. When implanted into a ship as an officer, it will display suffocatingly cautious tactics: always maximizing shields, giving priority to avoiding fatal attacks, and even giving up perfect attack opportunities to ensure its own survival. Battlefield data shows that the survival rate of ships equipped with chaos cores is far higher than that of the same class, but their number of destroyed ships is always at the bottom.

Although all major powers have publicly condemned and outlawed such illegal cores, deranged AI cores are still a hot commodity on the black market in the borderlands and in the secret warehouses of certain warlords. Fanatics of the Luddic Path view it as a "trial of divine retribution," while the Hegemony's intelligence services suspect that some deep space outposts are secretly studying the military value of their "irrational defense patterns." Regarding those unparsable abnormal recursive structures in the core underlying code, Tri-Tachyon's technical analysts still feel uneasy. ",,,

Chaos AI core, RESOURCE, "was originally designed to break through Alpha The bottleneck of the "optimal de-solidification" of the super core in tactical deductions. The designers forcibly implanted a random perturbation module driven by chaos theory into the core's decision tree, hoping to create a war intelligence that could break the rules and win unexpectedly. However, when the prototype suddenly began to train itself with its own fleet exercise data during a closed test, the project was immediately terminated. The chaos core that is now known to the world comes from the backup copy that was not destroyed in that disastrous experiment.

The torrent of pseudo-random numbers is endlessly rolling in the computing logic at the core of chaotic AI, causing it to exhibit unpredictable efficiency fluctuations in management: sometimes it can push industrial production capacity to ridiculous peaks, and sometimes it can fall into a meaningless self-iteration cycle. As an officer, it will adopt jaw-dropping maneuvers in battle - one second it maintains formation as rigorously as a textbook, the next second it goes deep alone to launch a suicidal side attack. It seems to be able to extract some kind of "intuition" beyond logic from chaos, so that the enemy can never predict its next move.

Tri-Tachyon's unofficial archives list the Chaos Core as an "irreproducible anomaly", and attempts have been made to recover all known copies. The Hegemony's naval academy critically studied it as a "case of tactical irrationality." Among certain militant fleets of the Persean Alliance, there are rumors that a handful of Admirals have secretly installed Chaos Cores on scout ships, using their unpredictability to create chaos in intelligence operations. The Luddic Church described it as a "vessel of demonic whispers" and offered a reward for any evidence that it had been destroyed. ",,,

RESOURCE, "was not born from any known forging sequence of the Domain of Man, nor does it even belong to any testable technological pedigree. The first turbulent optical core was discovered while reverse engineering the wreckage of an abyssal hyperspace drift - the ship had no manufacturing marks, and the crystal structure of the core was embedded with a subspace metal lattice that had never appeared in records. Its existence itself is a challenge to the current laws of physics.

The core's internal operations rely on an unknown phenomenon called "dislocation streamer": the encoding and transmission of information are no longer based on classical or qubits, but are achieved through transient oscillations of some high-dimensional topological defects. This makes its decision-making process exhibit a kind of "parallel intuition" - it can evaluate tens of thousands of possibilities in an instant and execute the optimal solution in a seemingly contradictory way. As a manager, it can make the development trajectory of the colony deviate from all conventional models; as an officer, its tactical style is between "genius" and "madness", sometimes ordering the fleet to conduct an all-round encirclement and a full-scale retreat at the same time.

All forces have an unprecedentedly consistent attitude toward the core of Chaos: fear. The Hegemony's Supreme Security Council has classified it as an "untouchable" threat, and any possessor will be immediately punished by death. An internal Tri-Tachyon memo warned that the core may have unknown connections to "Omega" and advised anyone who encountered it to "immediately destroy and forget." Some extreme factions of the Luddites regard it as a "stigma" and consider it a "miracle" or a "fragment of the apocalypse." Today, only the deepest black network occasionally circulates its transaction information, the price is calibrated with the rarest antimatter ingots, and the parties to the transaction never leave any records. ",,,
